quote.text: Man is least himself when he talks in his own person. Give him a mask, and he will tell you the truth.
quote.author: Oscar Wilde, The Critic as Artist (1891)

macro.title: What It Means For VWRA, Global Bonds, Gold & BTC

stories.kicker: The World · The Gulf War, Day 32
stories.headline: Oil Breaks $100 as the War Comes for the Tankers
stories.dek: Three ships were hit inside a single day — two in the Red Sea, one at the mouth of Hormuz — and the number the whole world watches finally gave way: Brent crossed a hundred dollars for the first time since spring.

stories.headline: A Younger Cabinet Is Sworn In Beneath a Cooling Wire
stories.dek: Monday's new-look ministry inherits a 5.7% growth headline — and a trade cycle already turning over underneath it, with export growth halving in a month and a central-bank decision due the same day.

stories.headline: Nvidia's Compliance Net Names Singapore Its Problem
stories.dek: The chipmaker has cut more than half its approved Asian buyers and now sends staff to inspect data centres in person — and the re-export loophole it is hunting runs straight through the island, where a chip-diversion case is already in court.

stories.headline: Turf City Tests Its First Private Condo Tomorrow
stories.dek: Dunearn House books on Saturday from S$1.475 million, brushing S$3,000 a square foot — the first private home ever launched on the old racecourse land, and a clean read on whether the school-belt buyer still pays up while the mass market cools.

stories.headline: Sony Slots a New Sensor Between the FX3 and the FX6
stories.dek: The FX5, announced Wednesday, brings a freshly-developed full-frame sensor, internal RAW and — a first for the Cinema Line — Open Gate recording, at a price that lands squarely between Sony's hybrid and its documentary workhorse.

stories.headline: Home Assistant Learns to Take Instructions in Plain English
stories.dek: The 2026.7 release lets you build an automation from what you want — "motion detected outside," "bedroom too cold" — and point it at a room instead of an entity ID. For a household migrating off Lutron, it is the feature that finally lowers the cost of moving.

stories.kicker: The Good Life · The Auction Room
stories.headline: The Strongest Watch Half in History Just Closed
stories.dek: Phillips sold more watches in six months than any house had ever managed in a full year — and a 99.8% sell-through with 40% first-time buyers looks, depending where you stand, like a market in rude health or one running hot.

stories.headline: Spider-Man Aims to Prove the Summer Was No Fluke
stories.dek: A week after Nolan's Odyssey filled cinemas with a three-hour Greek epic, Brand New Day opens on 31 July with presales the industry hasn't seen in years — the second test in a fortnight of whether the theatre is genuinely back.

stories.headline: It Was Never the Hours. It's the Relationship to the Screen
stories.dek: The best study yet built on children the age of an eleven-year-old finds that problematic use — the compulsion, not the clock — is what predicts trouble. It points a worried parent at a far more useful thing to watch.

stories.headline: Oil over $100 flips the Fed's question from cut to hike
stories.dek: A supply shock is an inflation shock: yields ran to year highs, the dollar firmed, equities sold off and gold caught a haven bid — with the July meeting and the MAS statement both landing next week. Market explanation and education, not investment advice; forecasts flagged as forecasts.

stories.headline: The Comet We've Been Tracking Is Older Than the Sun
stories.dek: Before it leaves for good, the interstellar visitor we first flagged in No. 49 gave up its age. New spectroscopy reads it as a fragment of a star system that formed — and likely died — billions of years before ours existed.

stories.headline: The Dating Recession Is Now a Numbers Story
stories.dek: Young adults are dating less, the apps are shrinking, and the cohort that grew up on them is the loneliest with them. For a parent of an eleven-year-old, this is a preview of the romantic world she will inherit — slower, warier, and offline.

stories.kicker: Fitness · The Science of Ageing Muscle
stories.headline: A Singapore Lab Finds the Switch Exercise Flips
stories.dek: Duke-NUS researchers identified a gene that rises in ageing muscle and tips its protein balance the wrong way — and found that exercise turns it back down. It is mechanism, not a pill, and that distinction is the whole story.

stories.headline: The October Rush Comes Into Focus
stories.dek: The autumn stack has firmed up — a two-night stadium run, Singapore's first F1 Sprint weekend, and three brand-new tables worth a detour. Book the clashes first.

stories.headline: The Pain Bag
stories.dek: The West wrote ten thousand words on the Labubu and missed the iceberg beneath it: a twenty-four-billion-dollar Chinese economy built on selling young people feelings, whose viral emblem is a bag deliberately worn until it hurts to look at. What China's youth are buying instead of the future their parents bought.
